questions.stem: Synonym of EMANCIPATE:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The automobile industry is shifting towards electric vehicles. Q: Battery technology has improved significantly in recent years. R: Charging infrastructure is being expanded rapidly. S: This transition will reduce carbon emissions.

questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Coal is formed over millions of years. Q: Under heat and pressure, peat transforms into coal. R: Dead plant material accumulates in swamps forming peat. S: This process is called coalification.

questions.stem: Synonym of PRAGMATIC:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Shakespeare is widely regarded as the greatest writer in English. Q: He wrote 37 plays and 154 sonnets. R: His works explore universal themes of love, power, and betrayal. S: His influence on the English language is immeasurable.

questions.stem: Synonym of HARMONY:

questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The printing press revolutionised the spread of information. Q: It was invented by Johannes Gutenberg in the 15th century. R: Books became more affordable and widely available. S: This led to increased literacy rates across Europe.

questions.stem: Rearrange: company / its / announced / expansion / has / plans / the
options: The company has announced its expansion plans
The company has announced its expansion plans
Has the company announced its expansion plans
Expansion plans the company has announced
Announced the company its expansion plans has
questions.solution: The company has announced its expansion plans

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the slope-deflection method, the primary unknowns are the:
questions.solution: Slope-deflection treats the unknown joint displacements (rotations/translations) as primary unknowns.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sedimentation tanks in water treatment primarily remove:
questions.solution: Sedimentation removes settleable suspended solids by gravity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever 2.5 m long carries a 10 kN load at its free end. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: Max BM at fixed end = W × L = 10 × 2.5 = 25 kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Contour lines on a map join points of:
questions.solution: A contour line connects points of equal elevation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The detention time of a tank of volume 300 m³ treating a flow of 100 m³/h is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The number of links in a 30 m metric chain is:
questions.solution: A 30 m metric chain has 150 links of 0.2 m each.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Camber on a road surface is provided mainly for:
questions.solution: Camber lets rainwater drain off the carriageway to the sides.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a fixed beam carrying a UDL w over span L, the support (fixed-end) moment is:
questions.solution: Fixed-end moment for a UDL is wL²/12 at each support.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The coagulant most commonly used in water treatment is:
questions.solution: Alum (aluminium sulphate) is the usual coagulant for removing fine suspended matter.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section modulus of a rectangular section 250 mm wide and 200 mm deep is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Marshall stability test is used for:
questions.solution: The Marshall test determines optimum bitumen content in bituminous mixes.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The slump test on fresh concrete measures its:
questions.solution: The slump test is a measure of the workability (consistency) of fresh concrete.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The superelevation for a design speed of 80 km/h and radius 400 m (e = V²/127R) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In an open channel, the specific energy is minimum at the:
questions.solution: The specific energy curve attains its minimum value at the critical depth.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If the fore bearing of a line is 45°, its back bearing is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The broad gauge of Indian Railways measures:
questions.solution: Indian Broad Gauge is 1676 mm; 1435 mm is standard gauge.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The degree of static indeterminacy of a single-bay, single-storey portal frame with both bases fixed is:
questions.solution: A fixed-base single-bay portal frame is indeterminate to the third degree.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A beam has overall depth 550 mm, clear cover 30 mm and 25 mm main bars. The effective depth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the IS 456 limit state stress block, the line of action of the compressive force lies from the extreme compression fibre at:
questions.solution: The centroid of the IS 456 stress block is at 0.42xu from the extreme fibre.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The principal stresses at a point are 100 MPa and 20 MPa. The normal stress on the plane carrying the maximum shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** On the plane of maximum shear the normal stress equals the centre of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\sigma_n=\tfrac{\sigma_1+\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 100 MPa, σ₃ = 20 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{100+20}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_n=60$ MPa.

figure.caption: Centre of Mohr's circle = mean normal stress

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The final setting time of ordinary Portland cement should not exceed:
questions.solution: IS limits the final setting time of OPC to 600 minutes (10 hours).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The average domestic per-capita water demand in Indian cities is taken as about:
questions.solution: A domestic demand of about 135 lpcd is commonly assumed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A pin-ended column (E = 210 GPa, I = 16×10⁻⁶ m⁴, length 4 m) has an Euler buckling load of about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A pin-ended column buckles at the Euler critical load with effective length equal to the actual length.
**Formula.** $P_{cr}=\tfrac{\pi^2 E I}{L^2}$
**Given.** E = 210 GPa, I = 16×10⁻⁶ m⁴, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{\pi^2\times210\times10^9\times16\times10^{-6}}{4^2}$ N.
**Answer.** $P_{cr}\approx 2072.6$ kN.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy grade line lies above the hydraulic grade line by an amount equal to the:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a fixed beam with a central point load W over span L, the magnitude of the fixed-end moment is:
questions.solution: Fixed-end moment for a central point load is WL/8 at each support.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a well-graded soil, the coefficient of curvature Cc lies between:
questions.solution: Well-graded soils have a high uniformity coefficient and Cc between 1 and 3.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A total station essentially combines a theodolite with:
questions.solution: A total station integrates an electronic theodolite with an EDM and data recorder.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Using Manning's formula with n = 0.02, hydraulic radius R = 1.2 m and slope S = 0.0016, the mean velocity is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Manning's equation gives mean velocity from roughness, hydraulic radius and bed slope.
**Formula.** $V=\tfrac1n R^{2/3} S^{1/2}$
**Given.** n = 0.02, R = 1.2 m, S = 0.0016.
**Working.** $=\tfrac1{0.02}\times1.2^{2/3}\times\sqrt{0.0016}$.
**Answer.** $V\approx 2.26$ m/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The centre of pressure on a vertical immersed plane surface lies:
questions.solution: Due to increasing pressure with depth, the centre of pressure is below the centroid.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 6 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 10 kN/m over its full span. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a simply supported beam peaks at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{8}$
**Given.** w = 10 kN/m, L = 6 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{10\times6^2}{8}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=45$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge

options: From the part to the whole
From the whole to the part
questions.solution: Surveying proceeds from whole to part to control accumulation of errors.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 6 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 15 kN/m over its full span. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a simply supported beam peaks at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{8}$
**Given.** w = 15 kN/m, L = 6 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{15\times6^2}{8}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=67.5$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The instrument used to measure horizontal and vertical angles is the:
questions.solution: A theodolite measures both horizontal and vertical angles.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The compound mainly responsible for early strength gain in cement is:
questions.solution: C3S hydrates rapidly and gives early strength to cement.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 5 m clay layer (Cc = 0.4, e₀ = 1) under an initial effective stress of 100 kPa receives a 60 kPa increment. The primary consolidation settlement is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Primary consolidation of a normally consolidated clay follows the logarithmic compression law.
**Formula.** $S_c=\tfrac{C_c H}{1+e_0}\log_{10}\tfrac{\sigma_0+\Delta\sigma}{\sigma_0}$
**Given.** C_c = 0.4, H = 5 m, e₀ = 1, σ₀ = 100 kPa, Δσ = 60 kPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.4\times5000}{1+1}\log_{10}\tfrac{100+60}{100}$ mm.
**Answer.** $S_c\approx 204.1$ mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rigid pavements are generally made of:
questions.solution: Rigid pavements use a cement concrete slab that resists loads in flexure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has cohesion c = 15 kPa and friction angle φ = 25°. By the Mohr–Coulomb criterion, its shear strength on a plane with normal stress 60 kPa is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohr–Coulomb shear strength combines cohesion with frictional resistance proportional to normal stress.
**Formula.** $\tau=c+\sigma\tan\phi$
**Given.** c = 15 kPa, φ = 25°, σ = 60 kPa.
**Working.** $=15+60\times\tan 25^\circ$.
**Answer.** $\tau=42.98$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The initial setting time of ordinary Portland cement should not be less than:
questions.solution: IS specifies a minimum initial setting time of 30 minutes for OPC.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stopping sight distance depends on the driver's reaction time and the:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The pressure at a depth of 10 m in water (ρ = 1000 kg/m³, g = 9.81 m/s²) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD) indicates:
questions.solution: BOD measures the oxygen needed to biodegrade organic matter, indicating organic pollution.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If LL = 50% and PL = 20%, the plasticity index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shape of the ideal transition curve used on highways is a:
questions.solution: An ideal transition curve is a spiral where radius varies inversely with length.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a floating body, stable equilibrium requires the metacentric height to be:
questions.solution: A positive metacentric height (M above G) ensures stable equilibrium.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An axial load of 150 kN acts on a bar of cross-sectional area 500 mm². The direct stress is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The CBR test is used in the design of:
questions.solution: California Bearing Ratio is used to design flexible pavement thickness.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The thermal stress developed in a fully restrained bar for a temperature rise ΔT is:
questions.solution: Restrained thermal stress = αEΔT, independent of length and area.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Disinfection of municipal water supplies is most commonly achieved by:
questions.solution: Chlorination is the standard method for disinfecting drinking water.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A hydraulic jump in an open channel forms when the flow changes from:
questions.solution: A hydraulic jump dissipates energy as the flow shifts from supercritical to subcritical.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a singly reinforced section (Fe415, M30 concrete, width 300 mm, effective depth 550 mm), the limiting moment of resistance (Mu,lim = 0.138 fck b d²) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a balanced (limiting) Fe415 section the moment of resistance is fixed by the coefficient 0.138 in $f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$.
**Formula.** $M_{u,lim}=0.138\,f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$
**Given.** f_ck = 30 MPa, b = 300 mm, d = 550 mm.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.138\times30\times300\times550^2}{10^6}$ kNm.
**Answer.** $M_{u,lim}\approx 375.7$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Superelevation is provided on:
questions.solution: Superelevation counters centrifugal force on horizontal curves.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a simply supported beam with a central point load of 16 kN, the maximum shear force is:
questions.solution: Each reaction = W/2 = 8 kN, which is the maximum shear force.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The combined correction for curvature and refraction in levelling (d in km) is about:
questions.solution: Curvature (−0.0785 d²) and refraction (+0.0112 d²) combine to about −0.0673 d² m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The coefficient of discharge Cd of an orifice equals:
questions.solution: Cd is the product of the coefficient of contraction and coefficient of velocity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has cohesion c = 15 kPa and friction angle φ = 30°. By the Mohr–Coulomb criterion, its shear strength on a plane with normal stress 120 kPa is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohr–Coulomb shear strength combines cohesion with frictional resistance proportional to normal stress.
**Formula.** $\tau=c+\sigma\tan\phi$
**Given.** c = 15 kPa, φ = 30°, σ = 120 kPa.
**Working.** $=15+120\times\tan 30^\circ$.
**Answer.** $\tau=84.28$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The continuity equation for incompressible steady flow in a pipe is:
questions.solution: Conservation of mass gives A1V1 = A2V2 for incompressible flow.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 4 m carries a central point load of 20 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A central point load on a simply supported beam gives maximum bending moment at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{W L}{4}$
**Given.** W = 20 kN, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{20\times4}{4}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=20$ kNm at mid-span.

figure.caption: Simply supported beam — central point load

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Poisson's ratio for structural steel is approximately:
questions.solution: Steel has a Poisson's ratio of about 0.3.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Curing of concrete is done primarily to:
questions.solution: Curing maintains moisture so cement hydration continues and concrete gains strength.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If E = 120 GPa and Poisson's ratio = 0.25, the modulus of rigidity G is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The activated sludge process is used for the treatment of:
questions.solution: The activated sludge process is an aerobic biological treatment for sewage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 5 m carries two equal 10 kN point loads placed 1 m from each support. The maximum bending moment (constant between the loads) is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Two equal symmetric loads give equal reactions; between the loads the bending moment is constant at $R\cdot a$.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=P\,a$
**Given.** P = 10 kN, a = 1 m, span = 5 m.
**Working.** Each reaction $=10$ kN; $M_{max}=10\times1$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=10$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The most common index organism for faecal contamination of water is:
questions.solution: E. coli is used as the indicator of faecal pollution in water.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bernoulli's equation is based on the conservation of:
questions.solution: Bernoulli's theorem expresses conservation of energy along a streamline.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 4 m carries a central point load of 40 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A central point load on a simply supported beam gives maximum bending moment at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{W L}{4}$
**Given.** W = 40 kN, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{40\times4}{4}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=40$ kNm at mid-span.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The coefficient of permeability of a soil has units of:
questions.solution: Permeability k has the dimensions of velocity, commonly cm/s.

questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 4 m carries a central point load of 16 kN. The maximum bending moment is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The actual size of a standard modular brick (without mortar) is:
questions.solution: The actual modular brick size is 190 × 90 × 90 mm; nominal size with mortar is 200 × 100 × 100 mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Closely spaced contour lines indicate:
questions.solution: The closer the contours, the steeper the ground slope.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The cross-sectional area of one 25 mm diameter bar is approximately:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The limiting moment of resistance of a singly reinforced beam with Fe415 steel is:
questions.solution: For Fe415, Mu,lim = 0.138 fck b d².

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If E = 120 GPa and Poisson's ratio = 0.3, the modulus of rigidity G is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shape factor of a rectangular cross-section in plastic bending is:
questions.solution: Shape factor = plastic modulus / elastic section modulus = 1.5 for a rectangle.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The water-cement ratio law was proposed by:
questions.solution: Abrams' law relates concrete strength inversely to the water-cement ratio.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point the major and minor principal stresses are 110 MPa and 50 MPa. From Mohr's circle, the maximum in-plane shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The maximum in-plane shear stress equals the radius of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\tau_{max}=\tfrac{\sigma_1-\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 110 MPa, σ₃ = 50 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{110-50}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\tau_{max}=30$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The classical bearing capacity theory for shallow footings was given by:
questions.solution: Terzaghi's bearing capacity theory is the standard for shallow foundations.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of span 3 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 5 kN/m over its entire length. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a cantilever produces its maximum moment at the fixed support.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{2}$
**Given.** w = 5 kN/m, L = 3 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{5\times3^2}{2}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=22.5$ kNm at the fixed end.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A venturimeter is used to measure:
questions.solution: A venturimeter measures discharge using the pressure difference between throat and inlet.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shape factor of a solid circular section in plastic bending is about:
questions.solution: For a solid circular section the shape factor is about 1.7 (16/3π).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point the major and minor principal stresses are 100 MPa and 20 MPa. From Mohr's circle, the maximum in-plane shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The maximum in-plane shear stress equals the radius of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\tau_{max}=\tfrac{\sigma_1-\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 100 MPa, σ₃ = 20 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{100-20}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\tau_{max}=40$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Tacheometry is mainly used to determine:
options: Horizontal distances and elevations without chaining
questions.solution: Tacheometry measures distances and elevations rapidly using stadia readings.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point σx = 80 MPa, σy = 40 MPa and τxy = 20 MPa. The major principal stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The major principal stress is the centre of Mohr's circle plus its radius.
**Formula.** $\sigma_1=\tfrac{\sigma_x+\sigma_y}{2}+\sqrt{\left(\tfrac{\sigma_x-\sigma_y}{2}\right)^2+\tau_{xy}^2}$
**Given.** σx = 80 MPa, σy = 40 MPa, τxy = 20 MPa.
**Working.** Centre $=60$, radius $=28.28$; $\sigma_1=60+28.28$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_1=88.28$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The permissible pH range of drinking water as per IS 10500 is:
questions.solution: IS 10500 specifies a desirable pH of 6.5 to 8.5 for drinking water.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The process of determining the relative elevations of points is called:
questions.solution: Levelling determines the relative heights (elevations) of points.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a void ratio e = 0.5, the porosity n is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A prismatic compass measures bearings in the:
questions.solution: A prismatic compass reads whole circle bearings from 0° to 360°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Rankine active earth pressure coefficient Ka for a soil with φ = 30° is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a beam section subjected to bending, the bending stress is zero at the:
questions.solution: Bending stress varies linearly and is zero at the neutral axis (centroidal axis).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hardness in water is caused mainly by salts of:
questions.solution: Dissolved calcium and magnesium salts are the main cause of hardness.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge through a section of area 2.5 m² with velocity 4 m/s is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Loss of prestress due to elastic shortening is most significant in:
questions.solution: Elastic shortening loss is significant in pre-tensioning and sequential post-tensioning.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The velocity head corresponding to a velocity of 3 m/s is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Flow in a circular pipe is laminar when the Reynolds number is:
questions.solution: Pipe flow is laminar for Re below roughly 2000.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Block shear failure of a bolted tension member involves:
options: Tension rupture on one plane and shear rupture on another
questions.solution: Block shear tears out a block by tension on one plane and shear on the perpendicular plane.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The principal stresses at a point are 80 MPa and 20 MPa. The normal stress on the plane carrying the maximum shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** On the plane of maximum shear the normal stress equals the centre of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\sigma_n=\tfrac{\sigma_1+\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 80 MPa, σ₃ = 20 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{80+20}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_n=50$ MPa.

figure.caption: Centre of Mohr's circle = mean normal stress

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Traffic volume is expressed as:
options: Number of vehicles per unit time
questions.solution: Traffic volume is the number of vehicles crossing a section per unit time.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rectangular channel 4 m wide carries 10 m³/s. The critical depth (yc = (q²/g)^{1/3}) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Critical depth in a rectangular channel depends only on the discharge per unit width q.
**Formula.** $y_c=\left(\tfrac{q^2}{g}\right)^{1/3}$
**Given.** Q = 10 m³/s, B = 4 m, g = 9.81 m/s².
**Working.** $q=\tfrac{10}{4}=2.5$ m²/s; $y_c=\left(\tfrac{2.5^2}{9.81}\right)^{1/3}$.
**Answer.** $y_c\approx 0.86$ m.
